document.title: AKTI ERP Failure Prevention & Codex Operating Doctrine
document.owner: AKTI leadership / Usman Minhas until formally delegated
document.purpose: Prevent AKTI ERP from repeating ARI, AKTI OS, plugin, and ERP IV failure patterns by forcing planning discipline, source-of-truth hierarchy, Codex operating rules, CI enforcement, and delivery-first behavior. This revision preserves the original doctrine and adds post-Phase-2 autonomous-run lessons. Version 1.1 adds the Phase 6B ticketability correction: planning PRs must not create missing repo truth by changing code, schema, runtime, generated, package, contract, registry, migration, frontend, backend, or validation-script files.
placement_recommendation: Summarize the operational rules into AGENTS.md
Reference it in ADR-0002: Codex Operating Doctrine
Do not treat chat history as source of truth after this is committed

one_sentence_doctrine: Blueprint v1.2 is frozen. No v1.3 narrative blueprint will be produced. Phase 0 begins with enforceable repo artifacts, especially Prisma-derived contracts and Zod schemas. Codex must work through Plan Mode, AGENTS.md, small tickets, tests, CI, and reviews. Evidence supports delivery; it does not replace working software. The WhatsApp cost problem must be protected by a parallel CRM until Phase 2C is live.

failure_diagnosis.core_pattern: Previous projects drifted from idea to architecture to peer review to more architecture, while working software remained delayed or unusable.
observed_failures: Architecture documents became the deliverable instead of working code
Large blueprints kept getting corrected, but each local fix created cross-section drift
Entity lists, RLS lists, event lists, blockers, indexes, and manifests were manually maintained and therefore inconsistent
Codex was asked to build from vague prompts and produced technically functional but operationally weak frontend/backend results
Frontend screens were left undefined, so UX became generic and practically useless
The urgent WhatsApp/Meta ad-spend problem was repeatedly pushed behind broad foundation work
AI/chat outputs were treated as architecture authority instead of being recorded in controlled repo files
Evidence packs and SoT-style documentation created a false sense of progress
Lifecycle PASS, READY, or zero-trust audit success was mistaken for executable ticket readiness
Ticket-pack planning resolved blockers by modifying real repo truth instead of marking candidates blocked or creating a separate control implementation track
Green CI was mistaken for scope approval when a PR mixed planning artifacts with schema, scaffold, runtime, generated, package, or validation-script changes
Mixed planning plus implementation PRs created false progress by making tickets appear executable only because the planning branch changed the base truth
root_causes: No screen contracts before frontend implementation
No protected timeline for the first business-critical module
Too much premature declaration of future phases
No branch/PR role-purity rule separating docs-only ticket planning from schema/runtime/control implementation
No explicit planning-contamination stop condition when ticket-pack planning needed real repo truth that did not exist
No hard rule that missing exact files, runtime MCR, schema/API boundary, validation command, or source authority must produce a blocked ticket instead of optimistic promotion
CI was treated as sufficient evidence even when the diff violated the approved planning or implementation scope

frozen_inputs.blueprint_version: AKTI ERP Modular Platform & Configuration Blueprint v1.2
frozen_inputs.rule: Blueprint v1.2 is planning input only. It is not a build spec and must not be expanded into v1.3 before Phase 0 artifacts are produced.
allowed_next_outputs: ADR-0001: Architecture Governance and Source of Truth
ADR-0002: Failure Prevention & Codex Operating Doctrine
disallowed_next_outputs: Another JSON blueprint revision before repo artifacts
A giant Codex prompt to build the ERP
A peer-review-only loop with no code artifacts

source_of_truth_hierarchy.authority: Table existence, table structure, relationships, migrations, database constraints
Behavioral contracts, Zod schemas, manifest schemas, Gatekeeper contracts, screen contracts, validation contracts
Module-declared capabilities, permissions, API routes, events emitted/consumed, dependencies, settings, menu entries
Derived metadata from Prisma and annotations; not authoritative for structure
Accepted decisions, rationale, tradeoffs, consequences; ADRs explain why, not final table structure

source_of_truth_hierarchy.source: active phase control docs, process docs, policies, standards, tests, and validation evidence
source_of_truth_hierarchy.authority: Active execution contract for the phase, bounded by higher structural sources
Root Codex and failure-prevention operating doctrine
Specialized ticket-writing doctrine extending this operating doctrine
Planning direction and scope context only
Drafting and reasoning support only; never final authority

conflict_resolution_rule: When two sources conflict, the lower rank number wins. If a higher-level implementation contradicts an ADR, update the ADR after review; do not let hidden chat memory decide.

entity_registry_doctrine.rule: The Entity Registry must not become another manually maintained list.
entity_registry_doctrine.source_of_truth: Prisma schema plus required metadata annotations
generation_strategy: Use Prisma schema as the structural source of truth
Use custom Prisma generator, AST parser, or companion metadata annotations to derive entity metadata
Emit generated registry artifact during CI
CI fails if checked-in generated registry differs from generated output
CI fails if a tenant-scoped table lacks organization_id, RLS metadata, or required indexes
entity_registry_doctrine.scope_rule: Declare only Phase 0, Phase 1, and Phase 2 entities in detail at first. Future phases get placeholders until their planning begins.
must_not_do: Do not maintain an entity-registry.ts file manually in parallel with Prisma
Do not declare Phase 6 entities in detail before Phase 5 reality informs Phase 6 design
Do not create future-phase Prisma or entity baselines inside ticket-pack planning PRs; create a separate schema-control implementation PR when repo truth is missing
Do not treat entity registry as a runtime module unless a separate manifest, API, tables, and purpose are explicitly approved
new_table_checklist: negative cross-org test added if tenant-scoped

business_urgency_guardrail.problem: Meta marketing waste from multiple adsets and WhatsApp numbers remains an active cost center.

temporary_operational_fix.decision: Use AiSensy, Interakt, or equivalent shared WhatsApp inbox/CRM in parallel until ERP Phase 2C is live.
temporary_operational_fix.purpose: Stop ad-spend leakage while ERP foundation is being built.
temporary_operational_fix.rule: Do not let Phase 0 and Phase 1 delay operational relief.
temporary_operational_fix.deadline_policy: A specific Phase 2C pilot target date must be recorded in ADR-0003 before Phase 0 is considered accepted. If no date is committed, the parallel CRM remains mandatory.

meta_approval_contingency: Start WhatsApp Business API/BSP approval early
Build Lead Desk Core without waiting for API approval
Use CRM/BSP shared inbox while approval is pending
If Cloud API approval is delayed or rejected, continue with BSP-supported integration path and keep Lead Desk messaging behind engagement capability interface

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: Another v1.3 or v1.4 document is produced before repo artifacts
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Stop. Produce AGENTS.md, ADRs, Zod contracts, Prisma-derived registry, or build spec instead.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: Codex is asked to build a feature without first inspecting repo and producing plan
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Use Plan Mode and require implementation plan before file changes.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: RLS lists, event lists, blockers, phase lists, and indexes are maintained by hand
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Derive lists from Prisma/manifest contracts and enforce with CI.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: Codex builds dashboards, tables, or forms without screen contract
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Block implementation until screen contract exists.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: UI shows generic cards, placeholder charts, or dummy states without operational value
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Require user task, primary action, filters, empty/loading/error states, and real API/mock contract.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: A decision is used because ChatGPT suggested it previously
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Record accepted decisions in ADRs and contracts; chat is advisory only.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: Task is marked complete because a report, evidence bundle, or SoT was produced
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Done means working code, validation, CI, and acceptance behavior.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: SCM, TDM, DEM, ESM, DFM, blocker registry, or zero-trust reports pass and the phase is treated as implementation-ticket ready without exact files, runtime MCR, validation, dependencies, and blockers checked against current repo HEAD
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Treat lifecycle evidence as compiler input for ticket writing. Promote tickets only after ticketability, ownership, dependency, runtime-MCR, validation, blocker, and freshness gates pass.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: A ticket-pack planning branch changes schema, runtime, contracts, generated registry, packages, scripts, migrations, frontend, backend, or validation wiring to make blocked tickets appear executable
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Stop the planning pass. Mark candidates blocked and create a separate schema-control, scaffold-control, decision-control, or validation-control implementation PR.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: A PR passes CI but contains files outside the approved role of the branch or ticket
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Quarantine or split the PR. CI proves technical checks, not scope legitimacy.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: A single PR contains ticket-pack planning artifacts plus implementation infrastructure and is treated as one clean deliverable
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Preserve the evidence if useful, but do not merge as final authority. Split into docs-only planning and clearly labeled implementation/control PRs.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: Phase 5/6 entities are fully specified before Phase 1/2 are built
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Keep future modules as placeholders until phase-specific planning.

anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.symptom: A module directly writes another module's tables or exposes unregistered APIs
anti_patterns_and_required_corrections.required_correction: Use manifest, registry, capability, and event contracts.

codex_operating_model.plan_mode_threshold: Use Plan Mode for at least 85% of interactions, and for every multi-file, schema, auth, permission, RLS, payment, certification, WhatsApp, frontend screen, or module-boundary task.
implementation_mode_allowed_for: Single-file text edits with no architecture impact

branch_pr_role_purity.rule: Every branch and PR must have one role: planning, control implementation, capability implementation, review, or documentation correction.
branch_pr_role_purity.planning_pr_rule: Ticket-pack planning PRs are docs/process artifacts only and must not modify code, schema, runtime, generated, package, contract, registry, migration, frontend, backend, or validation-script files.
branch_pr_role_purity.implementation_pr_rule: Schema, scaffold, runtime, contract, generated, package, migration, validation, frontend, or backend changes require a separate implementation or control PR with explicit scope.
branch_pr_role_purity.ci_rule: CI success never overrides the approved role of the branch or PR.

ticket_pack_planning_policy.rule: Ticket-pack planning reveals blockers; it does not resolve blockers by changing repo truth.
ticket_pack_planning_policy.missing_repo_truth_output: If schema, runtime, contract, validation, manifest, generated registry, permission, API, service boundary, screen contract, ADL interpretation, or business logic is missing, produce blocked candidates or a separate control implementation plan.
ticket_pack_planning_policy.promotion_rule: A ticket may become executable-review-ready only from repo truth already present on the accepted base branch or accepted dependency PR.

mixed_scope_pr_policy.rule: A mixed planning plus implementation diff is a scope failure even when CI is green.
mixed_scope_pr_policy.required_action: Quarantine, split, or replace the PR before using it as planning or execution authority.

worktree_policy.rule: Use worktrees for parallel bounded tasks only. Do not run two Codex agents on overlapping files.
safe_parallel_examples: one agent frontend screen using existing API contract
unsafe_parallel_examples: one agent changing registry while another changes manifests
frontend agent inventing UI while backend contract is undefined

mcp_policy.principle: Use MCPs only when they remove a real manual loop or provide needed external context.
approved_initial_mcps: GitHub MCP for repo/PR/issues if available
Official docs lookup MCP if available
do_not_install: MCPs added only because they look impressive
Tools that create new security exposure without real workflow value

skills_policy.principle: Create skills for repeatable AKTI workflows, not one-off prompts.

agents_md_required_content.purpose: Persistent Codex instructions for repo behavior, stack, rules, validation, and done criteria.

rules: Always use Plan Mode for multi-step work
Never hardcode organization, campus, role, group, or user names
Never bypass Gatekeeper for high-risk actions
Never add tenant-scoped tables without organization_id and RLS metadata
Never create frontend screens without a screen contract
Never create new UI patterns when packages/ui has a component
Never leave TODOs in production code
Never claim done without validation commands
Never produce documentation instead of working code unless the ticket is explicitly documentation

Never let ticket-pack planning resolve blockers by changing code, schema, runtime, contracts, generated artifacts, package files, migrations, frontend, backend, registry outputs, or validation scripts
Never treat a green mixed-scope PR as valid planning or execution authority
done_means: No hardcoded tenant or business assumptions

standard_codex_prompts.plan_mode_prompt: We are in Plan Mode. Do not modify files yet. Task: [one specific task]. Declare whether this is planning, control implementation, capability implementation, review, or documentation correction. Context: read AGENTS.md, /docs/adr/*, /packages/contracts/*, /prisma/schema.prisma, and only relevant module files. Required output: files inspected, current architecture understanding, step-by-step implementation plan, risks/assumptions, exact files to change, files forbidden to change, validation commands, acceptance criteria, and what will not be changed. If ticket-pack planning discovers missing repo truth, mark the candidate blocked or propose a separate control implementation PR; do not plan to mutate real repo truth inside the planning PR. Do not write code until I approve the plan.
standard_codex_prompts.implementation_prompt: Implement the approved plan only. Do not expand scope. Do not create new architecture decisions. Do not hardcode organization, campus, role, group, user, or AKTI-only assumptions. Do not bypass Access Core, Registry, Gatekeeper, RLS, or contracts. Do not leave TODO placeholders. Run the agreed validation commands. Provide a short completion note only.
standard_codex_prompts.review_prompt: Review this diff aggressively against AGENTS.md, ADRs, contracts, Prisma schema, RLS rules, permission checks, Gatekeeper usage, frontend usability, and scope control. Do not modify files. Return prioritized findings with severity, affected files, and recommended fix.
standard_codex_prompts.screen_contract_prompt: Create a screen contract before implementation. Include route, purpose, primary users, required capabilities, primary CTA, secondary actions, API/data source, table columns, filters, search fields, form fields, validation, empty/loading/error states, mobile behavior, audit hooks, and what must not happen.

forbidden_completion_claims: I could not run tests but it looks fine
Created docs instead of requested feature

frontend_prevention_rules.rule: No frontend screen may be implemented without a screen contract.
frontend_prevention_rules.why: Undefined frontend caused technically functional but practically useless apps.
frontend_done_means: Connected API or explicit mock contract
forbidden_frontend_patterns: Random dummy data not clearly marked
New UI pattern created instead of packages/ui reuse
Technical language exposed to normal users

codex_must_not_do_without_approval: Use AI-generated content to publish website pages without approval

ci_required_gates: Unit tests pass for touched module
No tenant-scoped table without organization_id and RLS metadata
No frontend screen route without screen contract

phase_0.goal: Convert blueprint into enforceable repo contracts, not another narrative document.
must_produce: ADR-0001 Architecture Governance and Source of Truth
ADR-0002 Failure Prevention and Codex Operating Doctrine

phase_1.entry_condition: Phase 0 contracts exist in repo and CI can validate them.
must_not_include: Full Website builder beyond shell if not planned
Future phase entity declarations beyond placeholders

phase_2.entry_condition: Lead Desk contracts, Engagement Gateway Lite interface, and WhatsApp contingency are accepted.
phase_2.business_goal: Solve one-number WhatsApp lead distribution and round-robin sales assignment.

architecture_decision_records.rule: No architecture decision is final because an AI said it. Decisions are final only when accepted in ADR and referenced by contracts/code.

required_initial_adrs.title: Failure Prevention and Codex Operating Doctrine
WhatsApp Protected Timeline and Parallel CRM Decision
Codex Plan Mode, Worktrees, Review, and CI Workflow

human_ai_governance.architecture_owner: Named human owner to be assigned by AKTI; interim accountable owner is Usman Minhas

stop_planning_when: The decision can be encoded in code
The issue belongs in an ADR
The issue belongs in a Zod contract
The issue belongs in a test
The issue is for a future phase
Ticket-pack planning would require code, schema, runtime, generated, package, contract, registry, migration, frontend, backend, or validation-script changes to make tickets executable
A PR diff mixes docs-only planning artifacts with implementation or control infrastructure

no_evidence_theater_rule.statement: Evidence is not delivery. Documentation is not delivery. Peer review is not delivery. Architecture is not delivery. A working, tested, merged feature is delivery.
no_evidence_theater_rule.ticketability_clarification: Lifecycle evidence is not executable ticket readiness. A ticket pack is executable-review-ready only after exact-file, runtime-MCR, dependency, validation, blocker, ownership, and freshness gates pass against current repo HEAD. Evidence that repo truth is missing is a blocker, not permission to create that truth inside the planning PR.
allowed_evidence: Screen recording for UI if needed
Audit artifact only when compliance task requires it
disallowed_evidence: Long evidence packs for normal feature tickets
Peer review as substitute for implementation

first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.title: Create AGENTS.md from doctrine
first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.acceptance: AGENTS.md exists at repo root and includes stack, rules, done criteria, validation commands, and forbidden actions.

first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.title: Create ADR-0001 and ADR-0002
first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.acceptance: ADRs exist with accepted source-of-truth hierarchy and Codex doctrine.

first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.title: Create module manifest Zod schema
first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.acceptance: packages/contracts/module-manifest.schema.ts validates a sample core module manifest.

first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.title: Create screen contract Zod schema
first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.acceptance: packages/contracts/screen-contract.schema.ts validates sample Lead Inbox screen contract.

first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.title: Plan Prisma-derived entity registry generator
first_codex_tickets_after_doctrine.acceptance: Implementation plan chooses generator/parsing strategy and CI drift check before code.

post_phase_2_doctrine_update.revision_note: Post-Phase-2 closure update. Filename remains v1; this section records lessons after Phase 1 PASS and Phase 2 PASS_WITH_ACCEPTED_DEFERRALS.

corrected_roadmap.name: First module-layer proof: Engagement Gateway, Lead Desk, and WhatsApp stub adapter

autonomous_operating_model.bounded_validation_repair_policy: Allowed only for deterministic local validation failures inside active ticket scope, with a finite repair budget and hard stops for scope, dependency, architecture, secret, or business-rule issues.
autonomous_operating_model.validation_test_runner_wiring_policy: Allowed only when wiring active-ticket tests into existing package validation without new dependencies or unrelated script changes.
autonomous_operating_model.exact_file_planning: Required before edits; broad globs are inspection hints only.
autonomous_operating_model.hard_stops: Use hard stops only for real blockers such as unsafe environment, forbidden file scope, new dependency, secret access, unclear migration strategy, invented behavior, direct WhatsApp/Meta coupling, frontend without screen contract, or repeated validation failure.
autonomous_operating_model.artifact_policy: Use lightweight per-ticket artifacts. Heavy packages are reserved for phase gates and final closure.
autonomous_operating_model.full_access_elevated_exception: The exception remains allowed only when explicitly bounded by active control docs for the current run; it is not retired and does not relax scope, validation, governance, or secret-handling rules.

post_phase_6b_doctrine_update.revision_note: Phase 6B ticketability correction. Filename remains v1; document version advances to 1.1 to record the planning/implementation separation lesson.
post_phase_6b_doctrine_update.incident: Phase 6B ticket-pack planning reached a state where capability tickets were blocked by missing schema and runtime baseline. The planning flow then created schema, scaffold, generated, contract, package, validation, and runtime-adjacent changes to make tickets appear executable.
post_phase_6b_doctrine_update.root_cause: Lifecycle artifact correctness and CI success were treated as sufficient ticket readiness. The branch and PR role boundary between docs-only planning and implementation/control work was not explicit enough.
post_phase_6b_doctrine_update.zero_trust_verdict: The real-code changes may be explainable as schema-control implementation, but they must not be merged as part of a ticket-planning PR or used to claim that the original planning pack was executable-ready.
required_correction: Planning PRs may create docs/process readiness artifacts only
Missing repo truth must produce blocked ticket candidates or a separate schema-control, scaffold-control, decision-control, or validation-control implementation PR
Control implementation PRs may change schema, contracts, manifests, generated registry, package scripts, validation scripts, and runtime scaffolds only when explicitly scoped
A post-control ticket-pack planning PR must regenerate executable tickets from the accepted base after the control implementation PR is reviewed and accepted
Mixed-scope PRs must be quarantined, split, or replaced even when CI is green
post_phase_6b_doctrine_update.phase_6b_recovery_rule: Treat the last docs-valid planning checkpoint as planning evidence. Treat later schema/scaffold/runtime changes as a separate implementation-control candidate, not as clean ticket-planning authority, unless split and reviewed under a dedicated implementation PR.
post_phase_6b_doctrine_update.future_phase_rule: No Phase 6A, Phase 6B, or later ticket-pack planning may promote executable tickets by modifying real repo truth in the same planning branch.

final_guardrail: If the next output is another broad blueprint instead of AGENTS.md, ADRs, contracts, or small-ticket code, the project is repeating the old failure pattern.
